subject: Re: Potential explanations for resolution not improving
body:
an update on this, if anyone faces similar issues:

I think my alignments are the problem. I used aretomo for reconstruction, 
but I see weird blurring and 'stretching' of signal in the x/z views. 
Fiducials, for example, make a C shape in x/z (rather than an X shape) in 
the reconstructions.

I suppose that M is not able to overcome such nasty misalignment and can't 
really correct the image and volume warp, etc. although I was able to 
refine ctf and get 1A improvement.
On Tuesday, May 30, 2023 at 12:06:01 AM UTC+2 joe dobbs wrote:

> Hi all,
>
> I wondered if anyone might have any suggestions for when resolution 
> doesn't improve in M for a particle that I know should improve. In other 
> datasets (with the same particle) M has substantially improved my 
> resolution, but in a new dataset it's only made extremely small 
> improvements: less than 1 A difference with poses, image warp, volume warp 
> and tilt angles turned on.
>
> I seem to be stuck around 13.5 A. I have some things I'll be trying, but I 
> wondered if anyone has any ideas about limiting factors that could be 
> stopping M from improving my resolution?
>
>
> Best,
> Joe.
>
